steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the text most strongly suggests about Crowley's work. The key tension: Crowley has devoted champions — Bloom read Little, Big 46 times and called it a 'masterwork' — yet he is 'not as well known as the most commercially successful American writers.' The gap between critical devotion and commercial reach is the signal.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'notable similarities to the work of James Merrill': The text mentions Merrill only as a champion of Crowley, not as a stylistic comparison. ✗
  • Choice B — 'not widely held among literary critics': The text names specific champions but never says critics broadly disagree; Bloom and Merrill are presented as influential, not fringe. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Bloom would have agreed it is superior to Merrill's work': No comparison between Crowley and Merrill's writing is made anywhere in the text. ✗
  • Choice D — 'some notable figures would agree it should be more commercially successful': Champions who call a novel a 'masterwork' and read it dozens of times would naturally want wider recognition for it. This is directly supported by the contrast the text draws. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
